metadata.summary: Foundational guide to MCP (Model Context Protocol) server development—building typed, schema-validated tools for Claude agents to invoke databases, APIs, and ML systems.

content.title: MCP Basics: Model Context Protocol Server Development
content.summary: Learn to build Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers that expose internal systems as typed, discoverable tools for Claude agents. MCP provides standardized interfaces, automatic schema validation, and secure credential handling—replacing ad-hoc shell commands with production-ready integrations.

why.p: Claude agents execute better when they have access to typed, named tools instead of improvising curl commands or SQL queries from scratch. MCP servers bridge this gap by providing a standardized interface: the agent sees tool names, descriptions, and input schemas. The server enforces permissions, handles errors, and returns predictable JSON.

MCP is particularly valuable for organizations building internal integrations: publish once to npm or PyPI, install everywhere, and all future Claude agents automatically get access to databases, APIs, and ML pipelines without modification.

list.item: Building a reusable typed interface between Claude agents and an internal system (database, API, filesystem abstraction).
Replacing ad-hoc Bash(curl:*) tool calls with a named, schema-validated MCP tool that other agents can discover.
Creating a Python ML inference server so Claude agents can invoke models as named tools.
Distributing a shared integration to a team via npm or PyPI — MCP servers are installable packages.
An existing public MCP server covers the integration — prefer npx -y @existing/package over building from scratch.
Single-use task where a direct WebFetch or Bash call is simpler and won't be reused.
The server needs to maintain stateful sessions between tool calls — stdio transport creates a fresh process per session, no session affinity.
Sub-10ms latency is required per tool call — MCP transport overhead is 10-50ms minimum.

section.name: Quick Start: TypeScript MCP Server
section.p: Get a basic MCP server running in TypeScript in under 5 minutes.

example.title: Setup
example.code: mkdir my-mcp-server && cd my-mcp-server npm init -y npm install @modelcontextprotocol/sdk zod npm install -D @types/node typescript

example.title: Minimal TypeScript Server (src/index.ts)
Build and Test
example.code: npx tsc node build/index.js # Test locally with MY_API_KEY=test

section.name: Quick Start: Python MCP Server
section.p: Get a basic Python MCP server running with FastMCP in under 5 minutes.

example.title: Setup
example.code: mkdir my-mcp-server && cd my-mcp-server python -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate pip install fastmcp

example.title: Minimal Python Server (server.py)
Test
example.code: MY_API_KEY=test python server.py

section.name: Full TypeScript Setup
section.p: Complete TypeScript project configuration for production MCP servers.

example.title: package.json
tsconfig.json
Basic TypeScript Server (src/index.ts)
Build and Test
example.code: npm run build npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector node build/index.js

section.name: Full Python Setup
section.p: Complete Python project configuration for production MCP servers.

example.title: Setup
example.code: mkdir my-mcp-server && cd my-mcp-server python -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate pip install "mcp[cli]" fastmcp

example.title: FastMCP Server (server.py)
Official SDK Server (server.py)
Test
example.code: python -m mcp dev server.py # or fastmcp dev server.py

section.name: Registering with Claude Code
section.p: Make your server available to Claude agents.

example.title: Via CLI
example.code: claude mcp add my-server -s user -- npx my-mcp-server # or with env vars claude mcp add my-server -s user -e API_KEY=$API_KEY -- npx my-mcp-server

example.title: Via settings.json

rule.statement: After registration, restart Claude Code. The new server will appear in /claude mcp list.

section.name: Testing Your Server
section.p: Verify your server works before deploying.

example.title: Interactive Testing with MCP Inspector
example.code:
# TypeScript npm run build npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector node build/index.js
# Python fastmcp dev server.py

example.title: CLI Testing (Headless Environments)
example.code: npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector --cli node build/index.js

rule.statement: MCP Inspector shows exact JSON input/output and catches schema mismatches immediately. For headless environments (CI), use --cli mode.

section.name: API Wrapper Template (TypeScript)
section.p: Common pattern for wrapping a REST API and exposing it as MCP tools.

example.title: TypeScript API Wrapper

section.name: Database Wrapper Template (Python)
section.p: Pattern for exposing database access as MCP tools with enforcement of read-only access.

example.title: Python Database Wrapper

section.name: Publishing Servers
section.p: Share your server with others via npm or PyPI.

example.title: Publish TypeScript to npm
example.code:
# Build npm run build
# Login to npm npm login
# Publish npm publish --access public

example.title: Publish Python to PyPI
example.code:
# Install build tools pip install build twine
# Build python -m build
# Upload twine upload dist/*

section.name: Limitations and Common Failures
section.p: Understanding MCP server limitations prevents surprises in production.

antipattern.description: TypeScript "type": "module" requires Node.js >= 18; silent runtime errors on older Node.
antipattern.reason: Pin Node.js version in .nvmrc or engines field in package.json. Test with your minimum supported version.

antipattern.description: FastMCP does not support streaming tool responses — use the official mcp Python SDK for streaming.
antipattern.reason: For streaming, use official mcp SDK. For non-streaming, FastMCP is simpler and faster to write.

antipattern.description: If process.env.API_KEY is undefined in the TypeScript server, the server starts successfully but all tool calls fail at runtime — validate env at startup.
antipattern.reason: Validate all env vars at server startup (before server.connect()), not inside tool handlers. Fail fast with a clear error message.

antipattern.description: Large tool return values (>50KB) are passed verbatim into the agent's context window; they crowd out other context.
antipattern.reason: Always truncate large payloads before returning — text[:10000] or equivalent prevents context window bloat.

antipattern.description: npx -y package@latest at Claude Code startup pulls the latest package version — a breaking upstream release can silently break all agents relying on that server.
antipattern.reason: Pin SDK versions in package.json and requirements.txt. Use "1.2.3" not "*". Pin packages in claude mcp add: npx my-mcp-server@1.2.3.

antipattern.description: MCP Inspector requires a browser (port 5173) — use --cli flag or direct stdin testing in headless environments.
antipattern.reason: Use --cli mode for headless/CI environments. For development, browser GUI is convenient.

antipattern.description: Python asyncio servers (async def tool handlers) cannot be tested with synchronous test harnesses; use asyncio.run() in test scripts.
antipattern.reason: Always use mcp.run() as the entry point for FastMCP servers. For official SDK, use asyncio.run(main()) in __main__.

section.name: Best Practices
section.p: Key recommendations for production-grade MCP servers.

rule.statement: Validate all env vars at server startup (before server.connect()), not inside tool handlers — fail fast with a clear error message.
Write tool descriptions as Claude would read them: "Fetches X by Y. Returns Z format. Use when you need W." — quality descriptions directly affect agent tool-selection accuracy.
Always truncate large payloads before returning — text[:10000] or equivalent prevents context window bloat.
Handle HTTP errors inside the tool handler and return them as text content — do not let exceptions propagate and crash the server.
Pin SDK versions in package.json and requirements.txt — "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "1.2.3" not "*".
Add a mcp-setup.sh idempotent registration script to the repo root — makes developer onboarding reproducible.

section.name: Agentic Workflow
section.p: Orchestrate agent teams to scaffold, test, and deploy MCP servers.

rule.statement: A scaffolding agent writes the server from a template, builds it, and runs MCP Inspector in --cli mode to verify each tool returns expected output. A registration agent then runs claude mcp add and writes a reproducible mcp-setup.sh script. The whole pipeline (scaffold → test → register → document) is sequential, driven by a single orchestrator. Human review at the "register" step is recommended for production servers with write permissions.

section.note: Recommended subagents: General-purpose implementer (Write, Edit, Bash) — scaffolds TypeScript or Python server from template. Bash testing agent — runs echo '{"params": {...}}' | node build/index.js for manual stdin testing. faion-spec-reviewer-agent — validates tool descriptions and input schemas for completeness.

example.title: Prompt Pattern for Scaffolding

section.name: AI-Agent Gotchas
section.p: Specific challenges when agents build and use MCP servers.

antipattern.description: An agent that builds a server and runs claude mcp add cannot use the new server until Claude Code restarts — the agent must finish and inform the user to restart.
antipattern.reason: Plan multi-session workflows when server installation is part of the task. Document that agents must restart to use newly-installed servers.

antipattern.description: MCP tool names are global within a registered server; if two registered servers expose a tool with the same name, Claude will invoke whichever it finds first — use descriptive, unique names.
antipattern.reason: Use namespace prefixes in tool names: {service}_{action}. Example: db_query, db_list_tables.

antipattern.description: If a Python FastMCP server uses async def handlers but is launched with python server.py (not mcp.run()), it will start but all async calls silently return None.
antipattern.reason: Always use mcp.run() as the entry point for FastMCP servers. For official SDK, use asyncio.run(main()) in __main__.

antipattern.description: Agents cannot introspect what tools a server provides without calling list_tools — the MCP Inspector does this; in automated testing scripts, call list_tools first to verify server registration succeeded.
antipattern.reason: In automated workflows, always call list_tools as the first validation step after registration.

antipattern.description: Return values must be serializable to JSON text content — returning a Python object (dict, list) without json.dumps() will produce a [object Object] string in TypeScript or a type error in Python.
antipattern.reason: Always call json.dumps() on Python objects before returning. In TypeScript, JSON.stringify() on complex objects.

section.name: CLI Tools and Services
section.p: Tools used in MCP server development.

rule.statement: npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector — browser/CLI GUI to test MCP server tools interactively. fastmcp dev server.py — Python live-reload dev server. python -m mcp dev server.py — official Python SDK dev server. tsc — TypeScript compiler. claude mcp add/list/remove — manage server registrations (bundled). npm publish — publish TypeScript server to npm. twine upload dist/* — publish Python server to PyPI.

MCP TypeScript SDK (OSS, native). FastMCP Python (OSS, decorator API, gofastmcp.com). MCP Python SDK (OSS, required for streaming). MCP Inspector (OSS). npm (SaaS, distribute TypeScript servers). PyPI (SaaS, distribute Python servers).

section.name: Agent Selection Guide
section.p: Choosing the right agent model for MCP tasks.

rule.statement: Use haiku for: Create new skill from template (mechanical task with clear structure). Use sonnet for: Configure MCP server for Claude (implementation with moderate complexity), Write agent system prompt (requires clear communication and nuance), Implement custom slash command (coding with context and interaction). Use opus for: Design agent architecture for complex task (requires reasoning about delegation and isolation).
